members.name: Bitbiashara
members.tagline: Empowering Tena estate entrepreneurs with Bitcoin education to unlock global opportunities for local businesses
members.description: Bitbiashara (Bitcoin + Biashara, Swahili for 'business') is a grassroots Bitcoin community project building a local circular economy in Tena estate, Nairobi. We actively onboard local merchants to accept Bitcoin, conduct regular education sessions, and host weekly community hangouts where people learn, earn, spend, and save in Bitcoin. Our approach focuses on simplifying Bitcoin through practical demonstrations so that anyone, regardless of technical background, can understand and use it confidently.
members.how_started: Bitbiashara began when founder Rosaline Wangui joined Bitcoin Dada and recognized the widespread misinformation about Bitcoin. As an entrepreneur herself, she set out to change the narrative by connecting with fellow entrepreneurs and demonstrating Bitcoin's practical value for business.
members.vision: To pioneer the future of business in Tena estate by empowering entrepreneurs with the tools, knowledge, and opportunities to leverage Bitcoin in their operations, demonstrating how Bitcoin can enhance business operations, reduce transaction costs, and provide access to global opportunities.
members.mission: To educate, empower, and support local entrepreneurs in embracing Bitcoin through practical education and user-friendly platforms, making Bitcoin accessible to everyone regardless of their technological expertise.

members.name: Afribit Kibera
members.tagline: Bringing financial inclusion to Kibera through education, merchant onboarding, and community programs
members.description: Afribit Kibera is building a Bitcoin-powered circular economy in Soweto West, Kibera – Africa's largest informal settlement where 80% of residents are unbanked. We create empowerment loops through our four pillars: Bitcoin education, sats-for-work waste management, women-led upcycling, and merchant adoption. We enable residents to earn, save, and spend Bitcoin locally, with 200+ residents educated, 50+ women earning weekly Bitcoin stipends, and 40+ local businesses accepting Bitcoin.
members.how_started: In 2019, Ronnie Mdawida and Yogi, working under the Ronnie Fund, asked a radical question: Could Bitcoin offer Kibera more than just payments? Could it provide financial sovereignty? Inspired by Bitcoin Beach in El Salvador, they recognized that Bitcoin requires no bank account, no ID, and no permission – a tool for saving that cannot be inflated or censored. What began as a small experiment has blossomed into the Afribit Kibera Circular Economy, a thriving ecosystem where residents earn, save, and spend Bitcoin locally.
members.vision: To build a self-sustaining Bitcoin circular economy in Kibera that brings financial inclusion, keeps value circulating within the community, and empowers local entrepreneurs – creating a replicable blueprint for informal settlements worldwide.
members.mission: To empower underserved communities by providing the tools and knowledge needed to thrive in a decentralized economy. Through Bitcoin education, earning opportunities in waste management and upcycling, and merchant adoption, we make financial freedom accessible to Kibera residents through sound money.

members.name: Bitcoin Githurai
members.tagline: Building a thriving Bitcoin circular economy in Toez Githurai through merchant education and community adoption
members.description: Bitcoin Githurai is building a grassroots Bitcoin circular economy in Toez Githurai, Kenya through a phased approach focused on merchant onboarding, community spending, and broader adoption. We onboard local businesses including cybercafes, electronics shops, barbershops, and restaurants, equipping them with Blink wallets, payment QR codes, and practical Bitcoin education through live transaction demonstrations and on-site community engagement.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Githurai began with inspiration and support from Bitcoin Beach, Bitcoin Ekasi, and guidance from Afribit Kibera. Recognizing the need for practical Bitcoin adoption in Toez Githurai, the team launched a phased approach starting with merchant onboarding. The first shops onboarded were cybercafes, where the team demonstrated Lightning Network transactions and Bitcoin's speed and low fees. From there, the initiative has grown organically, onboarding diverse businesses and building community awareness through education at each merchant location.
members.vision: To establish Toez Githurai as a thriving Bitcoin circular economy where local businesses and residents transact freely using Bitcoin, demonstrating practical financial sovereignty through grassroots adoption.
members.mission: To build a self-sustaining Bitcoin economy through systematic merchant onboarding, community education, and practical demonstration of Bitcoin's value as a medium of exchange in everyday transactions.

members.name: Bitcoin Dua
members.tagline: Empowering Agbozume's youth with Bitcoin literacy and financial independence to build a self-sustaining circular economy
members.description: Bitcoin Dua is a youth-focused Bitcoin education initiative in Agbozume, Ghana, centered around the Bitcoin Dua Education Center. We visit local schools to teach Bitcoin literacy, onboard merchants to accept Bitcoin payments, and host community events including football tournaments and workshops. Our goal is to build a self-sustaining circular economy where youth understand and utilize Bitcoin's value, supported by community farm operations that fund ongoing education and outreach activities.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Dua began with school visits throughout Agbozume, impacting over 1,000 students and teachers within the first week. Recognizing the need for sustained education, the team established the Bitcoin Dua Education Center as a hub for learning and community engagement. The initiative has grown to include merchant onboarding, community events, and a sustainable farm whose proceeds support ongoing Bitcoin education and development.
members.vision: To create a thriving ecosystem in Agbozume where youth are equipped with Bitcoin knowledge and tools, fostering a community empowered to achieve financial independence and shape its own destiny.
members.mission: To educate and empower youth through Bitcoin literacy programs, school visits, merchant onboarding, and community workshops that demonstrate Bitcoin as a catalyst for financial independence and economic growth.

members.name: Bitcoin Chama
members.tagline: Empowering rural Kenya through accessible Bitcoin projects like beekeeping and farming, building financial independence
members.description: Bitcoin Chama is a rural Bitcoin community in Kisii, Kenya, building a self-reliant circular economy through accessible economic projects. We empower young people and community members by introducing low-resource projects including beekeeping, poultry farming, and a community gym. Participants save their earnings in Bitcoin, protecting their purchasing power from inflation. We provide free clean water access, teach Bitcoin literacy, and onboard local businesses to accept Bitcoin payments, creating a self-sustaining ecosystem.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Chama was born from witnessing a friend's struggle with inflation. In 2021, he joined a motorcycle chama (a traditional Kenyan savings group) and saved Ksh20,000 over a year to buy iron sheets for building a house. When he finally withdrew his savings, iron sheet prices had doubled – his money could no longer buy what he needed. This realization led to the creation of Bitcoin Chama, where community members save in Bitcoin rather than fiat, protecting their hard-earned money from inflation while building towards their goals.
members.vision: To build a self-reliant Bitcoin community in rural Kenya where people live daily on Bitcoin.
members.mission: To empower our community economically through accessible projects like beekeeping, farming, and a community gym, while teaching Bitcoin literacy and encouraging savings in Bitcoin to protect against inflation.

members.name: Bitcoin Ekasi
members.tagline: Building a Bitcoin circular economy in Mossel Bay township through youth empowerment and merchant adoption
members.description: Bitcoin Ekasi is an extension of The Surfer Kids Non-Profit, founded in 2010 to empower youth in an impoverished Mossel Bay township where unemployment exceeds 50%. We pay our team of 11 surf coaches, teachers, and lifeguards entirely in Bitcoin while onboarding township vendors to accept Bitcoin payments, enabling coaches to spend their sats locally. We run daily Bitcoin education classes using the Mi Primer Bitcoin curriculum and operate a rewards program where children earn sats based on test results, fostering adoption through education and practical use.
members.how_started: The Surfer Kids NPO has operated daily for over a decade, teaching 50+ children surfing and commitment to long-term goals. Inspired by Bitcoin Beach in El Salvador, Bitcoin Ekasi was launched to build a circular Bitcoin economy within this established community platform. By paying coaches in Bitcoin and onboarding vendors, we demonstrate that Bitcoin adoption grows organically when built on a foundation of education and trust within an existing community structure.
members.vision: To create a thriving circular Bitcoin economy in Mossel Bay township that empowers residents with financial inclusion and the tools to build a better future through Bitcoin.
members.mission: To foster Bitcoin adoption through education and practical implementation, paying local staff in Bitcoin, onboarding merchants, and teaching community members to use Bitcoin as a tool for personal empowerment and financial sovereignty.

members.name: BitKwa
members.tagline: Building Ilorin's Bitcoin economy through education, merchant adoption, and peer-to-peer trade networks
members.description: BitKwa (Bitcoin Kwara) is a nonprofit Bitcoin-only community building a circular economy in Kwara State, Nigeria. Through our four core programs - Bitcoin Merchants Onboarding Project (BMOP), BitKwa Bitcoin Diploma education, The Bitcoin Hurdle meetups and conferences, and BitKwa Labs developer hub - we empower residents to earn, spend, and build with Bitcoin. We onboard merchants, train developers in open-source Bitcoin development, and create practical ecosystems where Bitcoin circulates within local communities, reducing reliance on inflationary fiat.
members.how_started: After running the BitKwa Bitcoin Diploma and educating hundreds of people, it became clear that knowledge alone wasn't enough. Learners understood Bitcoin but had nowhere to earn or spend it. Merchants were curious but lacked guidance. BitKwa evolved beyond education to build practical infrastructure: merchant onboarding, peer-to-peer trade networks, and developer training. Kwara State's strategic location, large informal economy, and youth population made it the ideal launchpad for Nigeria's first living Bitcoin circular economy.
members.vision: To build a financially sovereign society where individuals and communities in Africa live, work, and trade freely through sustainable Bitcoin circular economies.
members.mission: To empower communities in Kwara and beyond by driving real-world Bitcoin adoption through education, merchant integration, and circular trade networks, creating a self-sustaining economy powered by knowledge, innovation, and financial freedom.

members.name: Bitcoin NextGen
members.tagline: Empowering kids and teens in Cross River State with Bitcoin education through school clubs and earn-to-learn programs
members.description: Bitcoin NextGen is a bold educational initiative aimed at empowering kids and teenagers in Akamkpa L.G.A with the knowledge, tools, and confidence to participate in the Bitcoin economy. Through creative learning methods including school-based Bitcoin Clubs, spoken word, interactive books, games, and real sats, we teach children how to earn, save, and spend using Bitcoin. Starting from age 8 and up, we make Bitcoin practical, not just theoretical, helping build a generation of young leaders who are financially literate, tech-savvy, and Bitcoin-ready.
members.how_started: Bitcoin NextGen emerged from a recognition that kids and teenagers in Akamkpa LGA have limited access to formal financial education. The project aims to teach children about Bitcoin from a young age to prepare them for the evolving digital economy.
members.vision: To build a community where children understand money, freedom, and digital innovation through Bitcoin. We aim to create a generation of financially literate young leaders who are empowered, tech-savvy, and Bitcoin-ready.
members.mission: To empower kids and teenagers in our local community with the knowledge, tools, and confidence to participate in the Bitcoin economy. Through creative learning, hands-on wallet use, and circular economy activities, we help build young leaders who understand financial sovereignty and peer-to-peer technology.

members.name: Bitcoin Ekiti
members.tagline: Spreading Bitcoin adoption across Ekiti State through education and helping merchants accept Bitcoin
members.description: Bitcoin Ekiti is building a Bitcoin circular economy across Ekiti State, Nigeria through education, meetups, and merchant adoption. We run the My First Bitcoin Diploma program, teaching students, merchants, freelancers, and artisans about Bitcoin fundamentals and practical usage. Through community meetups, we create spaces where people learn, connect, and grow with Bitcoin while onboarding local merchants to accept Bitcoin for everyday transactions.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Ekiti emerged from a vision to bring grassroots Bitcoin education to Ekiti State. Led by Glory Ademilua, the initiative launched with community meetups and the My First Bitcoin Diploma program, bringing together merchants, students, freelancers, and artisans to learn Bitcoin fundamentals and practical applications. Bitcoin Ekiti creates educational spaces where adoption grows organically through knowledge and community connection.
members.vision: To establish Ekiti State as a hub for Bitcoin education and adoption where residents understand and use Bitcoin in daily commerce.
members.mission: To spread grassroots Bitcoin education throughout Ekiti State through diploma programs and community meetups, empowering merchants, students, and freelancers to adopt Bitcoin and build a thriving circular economy.

members.name: Bitcoin Ikorodu
members.tagline: Empowering Ikorodu's youth and businesses through Bitcoin education, merchant onboarding, and community events
members.description: Bitcoin Ikorodu is building a self-sustaining Bitcoin circular economy in Ikorodu, Lagos where residents earn, spend, and save Bitcoin locally. We onboard merchants with Lightning training and signage, run work-for-sats programs where youth earn Bitcoin while providing value, and host community events including school programs and monthly meetups. Our goal is to create a closed-loop economy where sats circulate within the community, demonstrating practical Bitcoin adoption at the grassroots level.
members.how_started:
Bitcoin Ikorodu was founded in 2021 by Destiny Smart. The project was initiated and inspired by the work of Bitcoin Africa Story, building on months of local engagement, education, and community-driven activities.

Starting with a small team and zero local merchants accepting Bitcoin, the initiative focused on introducing practical Bitcoin usage within the Ikorodu community.

Since then, Bitcoin Ikorodu has grown into a grassroots circular economy, enabling daily Bitcoin transactions through:

- Local educational sessions
- Merchant onboarding
- Community-driven programs

Today, the project continues to demonstrate how Bitcoin can function as real money; earned, spent, and sustained within a local economy.
members.vision: To build a self-sustaining Bitcoin circular economy in Ikorodu where community members earn, save, and spend Bitcoin daily.
members.mission: To empower local businesses, youth, and institutions in Ikorodu through merchant onboarding, work-for-sats programs, and school education initiatives that create practical Bitcoin adoption at the grassroots level.

members.name: Bitcoin Dombo
members.tagline: Empowering Mozambique's youth to build sustainable businesses through Bitcoin education and entrepreneurship training
members.description: Bitcoin Dombo is a Bitcoin circular economy based in Khongolote, Mozambique, launched by Maputo Skate and Education Social Project. We onboard local merchants to adopt Bitcoin as a payment method and savings tool while empowering youth to start and grow small businesses that accept Bitcoin. Through workshops on Bitcoin wallets, payments, and digital security, we strengthen the local economy by connecting merchants and consumers through Bitcoin, encouraging spending within the community.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Dombo was started by Maputo Skate and Education Social Project as a response to the need for financial inclusion and youth empowerment in Khongolote, Mozambique. Recognizing that young people needed practical pathways to entrepreneurship and local merchants needed alternatives to traditional payment systems, the initiative began onboarding merchants and training youth to build small businesses that accept Bitcoin.
members.vision: To adopt Bitcoin as a tool for financial inclusion and economic freedom in Khongolote, strengthening the local economy by connecting merchants and consumers through a Bitcoin circular economy.
members.mission: To empower young people and local communities to start sustainable small businesses using Bitcoin as payment and savings, promoting financial and technological education through workshops on wallets, payments, and digital security.

members.name: BitJR Academy
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin circular economy in Zambia through education and local merchant onboarding
members.description: BitJR Academy & Space is a financial literacy program in Livingstone, Zambia educating young people aged 6 to 17 about Bitcoin and digital finance. Through engaging activities and a dedicated learning space, we teach children practical financial skills and create a kid-driven circular economy where young people can earn, save, and spend Bitcoin independently. We empower the next generation to take control of their financial futures from an early age, shifting mindsets to recognize children as future builders and economic participants.
members.how_started: BitJR was created by Walusungu Nyachikanda, who grew up in Livingstone, Zambia witnessing how limited financial education was for young people. Recognizing that children were being left behind as the world moved into a digital economy, BitJR was launched to give young people practical knowledge and confidence with Bitcoin. Starting in Livingstone represents the belief that financial freedom should begin in childhood, and that the future of money belongs to every child who dares to learn and build.
members.vision: To empower the next generation in Livingstone and beyond with practical Bitcoin and financial skills, creating a future where children become leaders, innovators, and economic participants.
members.mission: To educate children and teenagers aged 6 to 17 about Bitcoin and digital finance through hands-on learning activities, building kid-driven circular economies that give young people the knowledge, confidence, and tools to manage their financial futures.

members.name: Bitcoin Victoria Falls
members.tagline: Building a thriving Bitcoin ecosystem in Livingstone through education, meetups, and merchant onboarding programs
members.description: Bitcoin Victoria Falls is a Bitcoin circular economy initiative in Livingstone, southern Zambia, focused on education, merchant adoption, and community building. Through the My First Bitcoin course, regular meetups, and hands-on merchant onboarding, we empower locals with the knowledge and tools to understand and use Bitcoin. We work closely with business owners to integrate Bitcoin payments into their operations, creating a sustainable Bitcoin ecosystem in the tourism capital of Zambia.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Victoria Falls was founded by Adam Cowperthwait after his transformative visit to El Zonte, El Salvador in late 2022, where he witnessed the Bitcoin Beach project's impact firsthand. Adam saw potential in the Free Haven Community Initiative in Livingstone, which shared similarities with The Hope House in El Zonte. He connected with Free Haven's founder Samantha Knaub and President Forest Hall, and together with Jason Peck, they began laying groundwork in early 2023. With support from Ndesanjo Macho and Anita Posch from Bitcoin for Fairness, they built the right team. In 2024, Bitcoin Victoria Falls officially launched with Humphrey Simwinga as community leader. The project started with the My First Bitcoin course and monthly meetups at Jollyboys Backpackers. What began as an idea inspired by El Zonte has grown into a real movement bringing Bitcoin adoption to Livingstone.
members.vision: To rekindle hope in southern Zambia by building a thriving Bitcoin circular economy where individuals and businesses can seamlessly transact, save, and build wealth using Bitcoin.
members.mission: To empower the Livingstone community through Bitcoin education, merchant adoption, and practical implementation – creating a sustainable ecosystem where Bitcoin becomes part of everyday economic life.

members.name: Bitcoin Anambra
members.tagline: Leading Bitcoin adoption in Anambra State through community education and building a thriving circular economy
members.description: Bitcoin Anambra is building a Bitcoin circular economy in Anambra State, Nigeria – home to Onitsha Main Market, Africa's largest market, and Nnewi, a hub of entrepreneurial activity. We conduct educational campaigns, regular meetups, and workshops at tech hubs to raise Bitcoin awareness while directly engaging merchants to accept Bitcoin for everyday transactions. Through merchant onboarding, community education, and sats giveaways, we're positioning Anambra State as a leader in grassroots Bitcoin adoption across Nigeria.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Anambra was founded by Kester Ejikeme, a passionate Nigerian Bitcoiner focused on demonstrating Bitcoin's practical use cases beyond just investing. Recognizing Anambra State's unique commercial strengths – including Onitsha Main Market, Africa's largest market by size and volume, and Nnewi's concentration of wealthy entrepreneurs and the Innoson Vehicle Manufacturing hub – Kester saw the potential to build a robust Bitcoin circular economy. The initiative started with educational campaigns to help locals understand Bitcoin's benefits, then expanded to systematic merchant onboarding in Onitsha's bustling market. Through regular meetups, workshops, and direct merchant engagement, Bitcoin Anambra is working to make Anambra State a leader in Bitcoin adoption across Nigeria.
members.vision: To transform Anambra State into a thriving Bitcoin circular economy, leveraging the commercial power of Onitsha Main Market and Nnewi's entrepreneurial spirit to position the state as a model for Bitcoin adoption and financial innovation in Nigeria.
members.mission: To educate and empower the local community through regular meetups, workshops, and outdoor campaigns while systematically onboarding merchants to accept Bitcoin – creating a sustainable ecosystem where Bitcoin is widely used for daily transactions.

members.name: Bitcoin Sisonke
members.tagline: Building Johannesburg's Bitcoin circular economy through education and merchant adoption
members.description: Bitcoin Sisonke is a Bitcoin circular economy initiative based in West Rand, Johannesburg, focused on education and merchant adoption. We conduct Bitcoin educational programs and regular meetups while encouraging local merchants to accept Bitcoin as a payment method.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Sisonke was founded by Retshepile as a Bitcoin education initiative in West Rand, Johannesburg. Starting with educational programs and meetups for people interested in learning about Bitcoin, the initiative grew as the team went deeper down the Bitcoin rabbit hole. Their knowledge and experience inspired them to evolve beyond education into building a sustainable circular economy, encouraging merchants to accept Bitcoin as payment.
members.vision: To build a sustainable Bitcoin circular economy in West Rand, Johannesburg where Bitcoin is widely adopted as a practical payment method and savings tool throughout the community.
members.mission: To educate and empower our community through regular Bitcoin meetups and educational programs while systematically onboarding local merchants to accept Bitcoin.

members.name: Calabar Bitcoin Club
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin adoption in Calabar through education, merchant onboarding, and a legal fight for Bitcoin rights
members.description: The Calabar Bitcoin Club was founded to introduce a parallel financial ecosystem where residents could address daily financial challenges including inflation, financial exclusion, censorship, and remittance issues. We promote Bitcoin adoption through circular economy building, education, merchant onboarding, and skills development. Bitcoin presents a solution for building a brighter financial future for Calabar – not just as money, but as a tool for financial sovereignty and economic resilience.
members.how_started: The Calabar Bitcoin Club emerged from the passionate Bitcoin adoption work of founder James Otudor. Recognizing the daily financial challenges facing Calabar residents – including inflation, financial exclusion, censorship, and limited banking access – James saw Bitcoin as a solution for building a parallel financial ecosystem. By aggregating all his adoption efforts into one powerful force, the Calabar Bitcoin Club was born. The initiative launched Bitcoin education programs in secondary schools in 2023, organized community meetups including a Bitcoin Halving Festival, and partnered with local businesses to accept Bitcoin. The club also advocates for Bitcoin rights through radio programs, newspaper articles, and notably, James's ongoing lawsuit against the Nigerian federal government challenging restrictions on Bitcoin access.

recent_posts.merchant: Chef Green Signature Local Cusine Hub
Chef Green Signature Local Cusine Hub
Chef Green Signature Local Cusine Hub

members.vision: To create a future for Calabar where individuals and businesses can confidently explore Bitcoin as a tool for financial freedom, financial sovereignty, and economic resilience. We hope to inspire the replication of successful Bitcoin circular economies in Nigeria and Africa.
members.mission: The Calabar Bitcoin Club is dedicated to fostering widespread understanding and adoption of Bitcoin across Calabar by educating residents, supporting local merchants, and building a strong circular economy on Bitcoin.

members.name: Bitcoin Aves
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin adoption in rural Akatsi, Ghana through merchant onboarding, youth education, and community empowerment
members.description: Bitcoin Aves is a grassroots initiative in rural Akatsi, Ghana building a Bitcoin circular economy through merchant onboarding and community education. We onboard local businesses – from barbers and tailors to food vendors, pharmacies, and electrical shops – showing them how to accept Lightning payments for everyday transactions. As professional teachers ourselves, we educate youth, students, and fellow educators about Bitcoin through community sessions, school meetups, and workshops conducted in both English and the local Ewe language.
members.how_started: In late 2024, in the small farming community of Akatsi in Ghana's Volta Region, a quiet revolution began. A few teachers, students, and merchants asked a powerful question: What if we could teach our people how to save, earn, and spend using sound money? From that spark, Bitcoin Aves was born in early 2025. As professional teachers in the Ghana Education Service, the founders are committed to helping their community escape inflation and financial exclusion through Bitcoin. They started by onboarding local merchants and hosting community workshops, making Bitcoin accessible and understood in both English and the local Ewe language throughout rural Akatsi.
members.vision: To see Akatsi become a leading example of Bitcoin-powered living in rural Africa, where shops accept sats, farmers sell their produce for sats, youths earn in sats, and schools teach Bitcoin as part of everyday life. We dream of a Bitcoin Circular Economy, built from the ground up and owned by the people.
members.mission: To empower individuals and communities across Akatsi and beyond through Bitcoin Education, practical adoption, and local leadership so they can take control of their financial future with dignity and confidence.

members.name: BTC Shule
members.tagline: Transforming Winteko village, Burundi through Bitcoin education, financial inclusion, and circular economy building
members.description: BTC Shule is building a Bitcoin circular economy in Winteko village, Burundi by integrating Bitcoin education, renewable solar energy, and community empowerment programs. We provide Bitcoin education to all households, reward community participation with sats, and support local entrepreneurs with Bitcoin-backed grants. Through vertical gardening for adults and Mi Primer Bitcoin classes for children, we create economic opportunities in a village with no traditional financial institutions or electricity access.
members.how_started: BTC Shule was founded by Belyï Nobel Kubwayo, who discovered Bitcoin in 2020 and became captivated by its potential to empower communities. As founder of Free Tech Institute NGO, Belyï integrated Bitcoin education into the curriculum to equip Burundian youth with digital skills. His vision transformed after attending the Adopting Bitcoin Cape Town conference in 2024, where he visited Bitcoin Ekasi, Bitcoin Witstad, and Bitcoin Loxion in South Africa, witnessing firsthand how Bitcoin circular economies create economic resilience in regions with high inflation. Inspired by this, Belyï launched BTC Shule (meaning "school" in Kirundi and other African languages) in Winteko village – a community with no financial institutions or electricity access. By integrating Bitcoin education with solar energy, BTC Shule aims to create a thriving, decentralized community that is financially and environmentally sustainable.
members.vision: To build a self-sustaining Bitcoin-based economy in Winteko that can be replicated across Burundi and Africa, creating financially autonomous and environmentally sustainable communities that thrive through decentralized financial ecosystems.
members.mission: To use Bitcoin and solar power to educate, empower, and uplift the Winteko community by providing access to essential services like electricity and financial inclusion that are otherwise out of reach.

members.name: Bitcoin Babies
members.tagline: Empowering Kenyan mothers with babies through Bitcoin stipends and a circular economy of 15+ local merchant partners
members.description: Bitcoin Babies is a foundation supporting mothers with babies under the age of one in Kimbo-Matangi, Kenya through weekly Bitcoin stipends and financial education. We provide mothers with an educational book covering infant nutrition and Bitcoin basics, empowering them to gain financial independence without relying on traditional banks. Through partnerships with 15+ local merchants who accept Bitcoin, we've created a circular economy where mothers can save, transact, and build financial independence directly within their community.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Babies began in 2024 in the Kimbo-Matangi community of Ruiru, Kenya with a group of 13 mothers. The idea was to send mothers small weekly Bitcoin stipends (around $5) to help with their babies' daily needs. However, the founders noticed mothers were immediately converting Bitcoin into Kenyan Shillings rather than using it directly. This raised a key question: What if they could spend Bitcoin without converting it? To make this possible, the team began working with local businesses in Kimbo-Matangi, encouraging them to accept Bitcoin as payment. The goal was to create a circular economy where Bitcoin could move within the community. Today, over 15 local businesses accept Bitcoin for goods and services, and a second group of 10 mothers joined the program in 2025.
members.vision: To create a world where mothers in underserved communities have the financial independence and knowledge to ensure healthy futures for their children, using Bitcoin as a tool for empowerment and building self-sustaining circular economies.
members.mission: To empower mothers with babies through weekly Bitcoin stipends, comprehensive education on infant nutrition and Bitcoin, and a thriving circular economy of local merchants – giving women control over their finances and creating sustainable pathways to financial wellness.

members.name: EcoBitz
members.tagline: Building Kenya's Bitcoin circular economy through merchant onboarding, grassroots community education and Recycling.
members.description: EcoBitz is building a Bitcoin circular economy in Machakos county, Kenya where Bitcoin meets environmental sustainability. We harness the power of Bitcoin to spark creativity through glass & plastic recycling, upcycling, and art projects that empower the local community. By integrating Bitcoin into our recycling and creative initiatives, we create economic opportunities while promoting environmental responsibility.
members.how_started: EcoBitz formally registered as Visionaries Recycling Initiative was founded by Kevin Makini in Machakos, Kenya to build a Bitcoin circular economy where environmental sustainability meets financial empowerment. Recognizing the intersection of Bitcoin and ecosystem preservation, the initiative integrates glass & plastic recycling, upcycling, and creative art projects with Bitcoin, creating opportunities for the community to Lean, earn and use Bitcoin confidently while promoting environmental responsibility in the local community.
members.vision: To build a thriving Bitcoin circular economy in Machakos where environmental sustainability meets financial empowerment, demonstrating how Bitcoin can drive both economic growth and ecological responsibility.
members.mission: Harness & utilize the power of Bitcoin to spark creativity and create channels for the community to earn Bitcoin through glass recycling, upcycling, and art – building a circular community powered by borderless money and environmental stewardship.

members.name: Bitcoin Famba
members.tagline: Fostering Bitcoin adoption in Maputo, Mozambique through meetups, workshops, and Lightning education
members.description: Bitcoin Famba is a grassroots initiative in Maputo-Matola, Mozambique empowering communities through Bitcoin education and circular economy building. Named after the Changana word meaning 'to walk,' we host meet-ups and workshops in Portuguese, onboard local merchants, and partner with Maputo Skate & Education and Lwandi Surf, to blend skateboarding and surf with financial literacy. We translate key Bitcoin resources into Portuguese, making financial sovereignty accessible to Mozambicans.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Famba was founded in 2025 by Daniel in Maputo-Matola, Mozambique to address the financial exclusion facing everyday Mozambicans. In a country where many remain outside traditional banking systems and face high inflation and systemic barriers, Bitcoin offers a solution - but only if it's accessible and relatable. The name 'Famba,' meaning 'to walk' in Changana, reflects the belief that financial autonomy is a journey beginning with knowledge, community, and courage. Bitcoin Famba started hosting community meet-ups and workshops in Portuguese, creating safe spaces to learn about Bitcoin's role in human rights and financial sovereignty. Through partnerships with Bitcoin for Fairness, Trezor Academy, HRF's Financial Freedom Program, Fedi, and collaboration with Maputo Skate & Education and Lwandi Surf, Bitcoin Famba blends youth empowerment with merchant onboarding, building a Pan-African Bitcoin movement rooted in local culture and language.
members.vision: To see Mozambique lead the way in Bitcoin adoption, education, and innovation - where financial freedom is not a privilege, but a right, and communities can thrive through knowledge, connection, and sound money.
members.mission: To empower communities through Bitcoin education in Portuguese, build a circular economy of local merchants, and make Bitcoin accessible and relatable through cultural storytelling - walking together toward financial autonomy and human dignity.

members.name: College BTC
members.tagline: Empowering university students in Ghana to learn, earn, spend and build with Bitcoin through campus circular economies
members.description: College BTC is a youth-led Bitcoin movement bringing circular economies to university campuses. We empower students to learn, earn, spend, build, and spread Bitcoin in real life, from onboarding vendors and running meetups to teaching Bitcoin development and creating peer-to-peer opportunities. Our mission is simple: turn students into Bitcoin-powered builders, entrepreneurs, and leaders who shape the future of money and digital freedom.
members.how_started: College BTC was founded by Bright Kportiklah as a student-led movement to make Bitcoin practical and transformative for university students in Ghana. Recognizing that many students face limited financial independence, rising inflation, and exclusion from modern economic tools, while Bitcoin remains misunderstood or inaccessible to youth, College BTC set out to change the narrative. The initiative started by creating grassroots Bitcoin ecosystems on campuses through the 5 Pillars model: Learn, Earn, Spend, Build, and Spread. By organizing meetups, onboarding campus vendors to accept Bitcoin, training students in Bitcoin development, and creating opportunities to earn Bitcoin through student services, College BTC transforms students into Bitcoin-powered builders and entrepreneurs who shape the future of money.
members.vision: To raise a generation of students who are financially independent, digitally skilled, and empowered to build with Bitcoin – creating campus-based circular economies that grow into global youth-driven movements.
members.mission: To spark a youth-led Bitcoin revolution on campuses by teaching Bitcoin development, empowering students to earn and spend in Bitcoin, and building real peer-to-peer economies that give young people financial freedom and a voice in shaping the future.

members.name: Bitcoin Tofo
members.tagline: Bringing Bitcoin to Tofo beach community in Mozambique through merchant adoption and grassroots education
members.description: Bitcoin Tofo is building a Bitcoin circular economy in Tofo Beach, Inhambane – positioning this vibrant Mozambican coastal community as the 'Bitcoin Beach of Mozambique.' We onboard local merchants including guesthouses, restaurants, dive shops, and surf rentals to accept Bitcoin, creating a value loop where tourists spend sats and businesses circulate them locally. Through education in Portuguese and local Bitonga language, we empower merchants, residents, and visitors to use Bitcoin for everyday transactions, strengthening local commerce and financial inclusion.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Tofo emerged as a grassroots initiative to bring Bitcoin adoption to Tofo Beach, one of Mozambique's vibrant coastal communities known for diving, surfing, and tourism. Recognizing that Tofo's natural tourism draw could be combined with Bitcoin to create a circular economy, the initiative began onboarding local merchants – guesthouses, restaurants, dive shops, and surf rentals – to accept Bitcoin payments. The model is straightforward: tourists bring Bitcoin via Lightning, spend sats at local businesses, and those businesses circulate sats within the local ecosystem rather than converting everything to fiat. By emphasizing education in Portuguese and the local Bitonga language, Bitcoin Tofo builds financial inclusion and sovereignty in a context where banking infrastructure is less robust. The goal is simple: make Tofo a destination where Bitcoin flows as naturally as the ocean tides.
members.vision: To position Tofo as the Bitcoin Beach of Mozambique – a destination where coastal tourism, local commerce, and self-sovereign money converge, creating a thriving circular economy that keeps value flowing within the community.
members.mission: To build a self-sustaining Bitcoin circular economy in Tofo by onboarding merchants to accept sats, educating the community in local languages, and attracting Bitcoin tourism – creating value loops that strengthen local commerce and financial sovereignty.

members.name: Soweto BTC
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin adoption in Soweto's township through merchant onboarding and education
members.description: Soweto BTC is a non-profit initiative providing Bitcoin financial education to underprivileged youth in Soweto, South Africa while supporting local small and medium-sized businesses. We run an after-school financial literacy program for kids aged 10-15 at our Bitcoin learning center in Meadowlands Zone 9, incentivizing learners with sats rewards every two weeks. By teaching youth Bitcoin fundamentals and helping local businesses adopt Bitcoin payments, we're building a self-sustaining circular economy that empowers both the next generation and local commerce.
members.how_started: Soweto BTC was founded in February 2024 by Michael Rathebe as a Bitcoin-only education and mentorship program targeting underprivileged youth in Soweto. Inspired by his attendance at the January 2024 Adopting Bitcoin Cape Town Conference, where he connected with Hermann Vivier of Bitcoin Ekasi and explored Bitcoin-driven economies in Mossel Bay and Witsand, Michael launched Soweto BTC to bring similar opportunities to his community. With support from Josef Tetek of Trezor Academy, the program held its first meetup in March 2024. Since then, Soweto BTC has conducted successful Trezor Academy sessions engaging over 80 participants. In January 2025, Michael and five teenagers from Thaba-Jabula secondary school spoke at Adopting Bitcoin Cape Town. This experience inspired the establishment of a Bitcoin learning center in Meadowlands Zone 9, offering after-school financial literacy programs where kids earn sats rewards.
members.vision: To create a self-sustaining, inclusive financial ecosystem in Soweto where Bitcoin enhances economic opportunities and resilience for youth and local businesses alike.
members.mission: To empower Soweto's underprivileged youth with Bitcoin financial education for financial sovereignty while assisting local SMEs in adopting Bitcoin payments – fostering a local Bitcoin circular economy that reduces reliance on traditional financial systems.

members.name: Bitcoin Loxion
members.tagline: Empowering Khayelitsha township through Bitcoin education, merchant adoption, and a garden project with Bitcoin stipends
members.description: Bitcoin Loxion is building a Bitcoin circular economy in Khayelitsha, one of South Africa's largest townships, through education, merchant adoption, and community empowerment. We host Bitcoin meetups and structured learning programs including Mi Primer Bitcoin and Trezor Academy, while onboarding local shops to accept Bitcoin payments. Through our Garden of Hope community garden project, participants grow food and earn Bitcoin stipends, demonstrating how Bitcoin can be a tool for growth and self-sufficiency.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Loxion was founded in 2024 by Thulisa Sikolpati, who was initially skeptical about Bitcoin due to stigma in her Khayelitsha community. Her perspective shifted after attending the Adopting Bitcoin Cape Town conference in 2024, where she witnessed Bitcoin Ekasi using Bitcoin as a tool for change and empowerment. With help from Bitcoin Dada, Thulisa recognized how Bitcoin could address global challenges and saw the urgent need for education in her township. She began hosting Bitcoin meetups in Khayelitsha, revealing significant gaps in understanding. The community transitioned to structured learning with Mi Primer Bitcoin and Trezor Academy, while local shops were onboarded to accept Bitcoin payments. To demonstrate real success, Bitcoin Loxion initiated the Garden of Hope, a community garden where participants grow food and earn Bitcoin stipends, teaching the community to use Bitcoin as a tool for growth and self-sufficiency.
members.vision: To transform Khayelitsha into a thriving hub of innovation and financial empowerment through a Bitcoin circular economy – a community where every individual has access to decentralized opportunities, fostering resilience and collaboration.
members.mission: To empower Khayelitsha by creating a Bitcoin circular economy that transforms lives through financial freedom and decentralized opportunities, promoting community-driven collaboration and building toward a dedicated community hub for Bitcoin education and empowerment.

members.name: Bitcoin Karoo
members.tagline: Supporting Karoo's small-town businesses and residents with Bitcoin education and sound money principles
members.description: Bitcoin Karoo is a circular economy based in the Karoo region of South Africa, a semi-desert area dominated by farming, agriculture, and small towns with owner-managed businesses. We educate merchants and residents in the principles of sound money, helping Karoo businesses struggling under broken money systems, rising costs, capital controls, and regulation. Our project guides and protects Karoo residents and businesses through the transition to a global Bitcoin standard.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Karoo was founded by Sarah Joubert to address the financial challenges facing the Karoo region's small-town businesses and farming communities. Recognizing that owner-managed businesses in this semi-desert region struggle under broken money systems, rising costs, and capital controls, Sarah saw Bitcoin as a solution for building financial resilience. Bitcoin Karoo focuses on educating merchants and residents about sound money principles and guiding them through the transition to a Bitcoin standard.
members.vision: To empower Karoo residents and businesses with financial sovereignty through Bitcoin education, helping them navigate the transition to a global Bitcoin standard with confidence and understanding.
members.mission: To educate merchants and residents on fiat monetary debasement, the core principles of the Bitcoin protocol, and the safe accumulation and self-custody of Bitcoin – standing for self-sovereignty and education first.

members.name: Bit-Fitness
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin awareness in Somerset West through fitness education and community health initiatives
members.description: Bit-Fitness drives Bitcoin adoption in Somerset West, South Africa by merging Bitcoin literacy with physical wellness and community building. We organize outdoor fitness activities, football tournaments with Bitcoin prize incentives, and host regular Bitcoin education workshops and meetups. Through grassroots programs, we orange-pill locals, support businesses accepting Bitcoin payments, help onboard merchants, and demonstrate real Bitcoin usage in everyday expenses – from groceries and bills to fitness services priced in sats.
members.how_started: Bit-Fitness was founded by Blaise in Somerset West to merge Bitcoin literacy with physical wellness and community building. Recognizing the power of sports and fitness to bring communities together, Blaise created programs that teach Bitcoin fundamentals through engaging activities like football tournaments, fitness challenges, and outdoor events. By hosting Bitcoin education workshops, collaborating with projects like Bitcoin Loxion and Bitcoin Africa Story, and organizing community initiatives such as Mandela Day meetups and Trezor wallet education sessions, Bit-Fitness demonstrates how Bitcoin can be integrated into everyday life. The initiative orange-pills locals, helps businesses accept Bitcoin payments, and proves that Bitcoin adoption can grow through sports, fitness, and healthy living – pricing all fitness services in sats.
members.vision: To empower communities with both financial freedom and healthy living, creating a model where Bitcoin adoption grows through fitness, sports, and outdoor activities that inspire youth and families alike.
members.mission: To drive grassroots Bitcoin adoption by organizing fitness challenges, football tournaments, and outdoor activities that teach Bitcoin literacy while building strong, healthy communities – making Bitcoin practical and accessible through sport and wellness.

members.name: Bitcoin Arusha
members.tagline: Building Bitcoin adoption in Arusha through cultural expression, combining education with music, dance, and events
members.description: Bitcoin Arusha is an innovative initiative merging Bitcoin education with music, dance, and digital media to create an immersive peer-to-peer entertainment experience in Arusha, Tanzania. By leveraging the power of art and community, we cultivate a sustainable Bitcoin circular economy that fosters real-world Bitcoin transactions. Through monthly educational classes, networking events, and creative cultural experiences including dance battle events, we empower artists, entrepreneurs, and professionals to integrate Bitcoin into their daily lives.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Arusha was founded by Don Kweka in late 2024, inspired by Bitcoin Ekasi and with support and guidance from Bitcoin Kampala. Recognizing the need for practical Bitcoin use cases and awareness in Tanzania, Don launched monthly educational classes teaching 20-30 students Bitcoin fundamentals and established regular networking events to build a foundation for adoption. By integrating Bitcoin with music, dance, and digital media, Bitcoin Arusha redefines how people interact with Bitcoin beyond investment – making it a functional tool for everyday transactions. The initiative produces Bitcoin-themed music, dance, and visual storytelling, and hosts dance battle events combining entertainment with real Bitcoin transactions.
members.vision: To establish Arusha as a thriving hub for Bitcoin adoption by integrating Bitcoin into cultural expression, commerce, and education – creating a community where Bitcoin fuels the exchange of creativity and value.
members.mission: To educate and empower individuals through accessible Bitcoin learning opportunities, build a sustainable Bitcoin circular economy through real-world transactions, and connect professionals, artists, and businesses through strategic networking and cultural events that make Bitcoin a functional tool for everyday life.

members.name: Bitcoin Kampala
members.tagline: Building Uganda's Bitcoin circular economy at Makerere University and supporting orphans through education and commerce
members.description: Bitcoin Kampala is building a grassroots Bitcoin circular economy in Uganda centered around Makerere University and supporting the Orphans of Uganda in Bugiri. At Makerere, the initiative enables students to use Bitcoin for tuition and supplies while merchants participate in a growing campus economy, and programs like 'Fruits and Eggs for Sats' introduce children to Bitcoin while ensuring nutrition. Through Bitcoin donations, the project has funded education, clean water, renewable energy, and farming land – proving Bitcoin's potential for empowerment across Uganda.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Kampala was founded by Brindon Mwiine in 2023, inspired by Bitcoin Ekasi and Bitcoin Beach. A month before BTC Prague 2023, Bitcoin Ekasi issued a challenge: build a Bitcoin circular economy in your community and win a conference ticket. Through a Twitter poll, Brindon emerged as winner, demonstrating his efforts organizing Bitcoin meetups and educating his community. He fundraised through Geyser Fund to cover expenses, secured a late visa, and arrived in Prague on the morning of the conference. The knowledge gained from Bitcoin Ekasi and Bitcoin Beach proved invaluable. Since July 2023, Bitcoin Kampala has onboarded vendors, schools, and orphanages to use Bitcoin as real money – establishing a thriving ecosystem at Makerere University where students pay tuition in Bitcoin, while supporting the Orphans of Uganda in Bugiri with education, nutrition, and sustainable farming.
members.vision: To create a sustainable and unrestricted Bitcoin circular economy in Uganda that empowers communities through financial sovereignty, education, and social good.
members.mission: To build a thriving Bitcoin ecosystem at Makerere University and support vulnerable communities like the Orphans of Uganda through education, commerce, and sustainable programs that demonstrate Bitcoin's potential as a tool for empowerment and transformation.

members.name: Bitcoin Plett
members.tagline: Building Plettenberg Bay's Bitcoin circular economy with 100+ merchants and self-custody education principles
members.description: Bitcoin Plett is building a circular Bitcoin economy in Plettenberg Bay, South Africa with a network of over 100 Bitcoin-accepting merchants. We host regular meetups, run community events, and provide education to help locals understand and use Bitcoin while our team of passionate volunteers works directly with businesses to onboard them as merchants. By collaborating with neighboring communities to extend Bitcoin adoption into underserved areas, we create a network where value stays in the community, offering tools for financial inclusion and long-term sustainability.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Plett was founded by Lorraine Leibbrand to build a circular Bitcoin economy in Plettenberg Bay, South Africa. Recognizing the need for practical Bitcoin adoption in the coastal town and surrounding areas, Lorraine assembled a team of passionate volunteers who work directly with local businesses to onboard them as Bitcoin-accepting merchants. Through regular meetups, community events, and education programs, Bitcoin Plett has grown to support over 100 merchants, creating a network where value stays in the community while collaborating with neighboring communities to extend Bitcoin adoption into underserved areas.
members.vision: To establish Plettenberg Bay as a thriving Bitcoin circular economy where value stays in the community through a network of Bitcoin-accepting merchants and educated residents who understand and use Bitcoin.
members.mission: To connect Plettenberg Bay through Bitcoin by hosting meetups and events, providing community education, onboarding local businesses as Bitcoin-accepting merchants, and collaborating with neighboring communities to extend Bitcoin adoption into underserved areas for financial inclusion and sustainability.

members.name: Bitcoin Ubuntu
members.tagline: Bitcoin-only education and onboarding in Swellendam, providing free resources and community events
members.description: Bitcoin Ubuntu is a volunteer organization in Swellendam, South Africa providing free, Bitcoin-only educational resources to local and online communities. As a light node with My First Bitcoin, we've delivered the Bitcoin Diploma curriculum to more than 10 cohorts, contributing to education projects in Kenya, Nigeria, and Malawi while making our adapted online curriculum freely available. Through local meetups and workshops, we've helped charities and businesses in Swellendam accept Bitcoin, embodying the Ubuntu philosophy of 'I am because we are.'
members.how_started:
Bitcoin Ubuntu was founded by Glenn Jooste in 2023 with a desire to provide free, high-quality, Bitcoin-only educational resources to the local Swellendam community and beyond. Recognizing that people needed better tools to avoid fraudulent schemes and defend themselves against irresponsible government monetary and fiscal policies, Glenn established Bitcoin Ubuntu as a volunteer organization. 

The initiative became a light node with My First Bitcoin, delivering the Bitcoin Diploma curriculum to more than 10 cohorts both in-person and online. By adapting the curriculum for online presentation and making it freely available, Bitcoin Ubuntu has contributed to education projects in Kenya, Nigeria, Malawi, and beyond while helping local charities and businesses in Swellendam adopt Bitcoin.
members.vision: To empower individuals and communities through Bitcoin education, promoting financial literacy and inclusion while embodying the philosophy of Ubuntu – recognizing that we grow stronger through communal learning and shared growth.
members.mission: To provide free Bitcoin education and onboarding resources through online education, consultations, meetups, merchant and charity onboarding, and operating a Mi Primer Bitcoin light node – integrating the philosophy of Ubuntu into all our educational efforts.

members.name: Bitcoin Witsand
members.tagline: Guiding Witsand's coastal village through the transition to a Bitcoin standard with education and self-custody
members.description: Bitcoin Witsand is a grassroots Bitcoin circular economy in the tiny coastal village of Witsand, South Africa – the unofficial Bitcoin Capital of South Africa. In this real-world example of a working Bitcoin circular economy, residents and visitors can buy everything from fuel to fishing tackle, restaurant meals to accommodation, massages to pet sitting, art classes to kite surfing lessons – all in sats. This community demonstrates what the future could look like on a Bitcoin standard, where Bitcoin flows naturally through everyday transactions in a scenic South Cape village.
members.how_started: Bitcoin Witsand was founded by Edwin Jones to transform this tiny coastal village in the South Cape into a living example of a Bitcoin circular economy. Recognizing Witsand's potential as a scenic destination with close-knit community businesses, Edwin began educating residents about Bitcoin and systematically onboarding local merchants. What started as a vision has grown into the unofficial Bitcoin Capital of South Africa, where residents and visitors alike can spend Bitcoin on everything from fuel and fishing tackle to restaurant meals, accommodation, and even kite surfing lessons. Bitcoin Witsand demonstrates that a real-world Bitcoin circular economy works, showing what the future could look like when communities embrace sound money principles.
members.vision: To establish Witsand as a living example of what the future looks like on a Bitcoin standard – a thriving coastal community where Bitcoin is the natural choice for everyday transactions and economic exchange.
members.mission: To guide Witsand's coastal village through the transition to a Bitcoin standard by educating residents, onboarding local businesses, and demonstrating that a real-world Bitcoin circular economy works – proving that sound money principles can transform communities.

members.name: Bitfiasi Initiative
members.tagline: Empowering communities with Bitcoin through education, access, and practical use
members.description:
Bitfiasi helps people understand and use Bitcoin in real life. Based in Dodowa, Ghana, we run programs like Bitcoin for Beginners, workshops, webinars, meetups, and merchant onboarding. We are working towards bringing merchants together to trade in Bitcoin and grow a circular economy, with early efforts already underway. We are also developing initiatives like the Bitcoin Booth to expand access and real use.

Our approach is community-first. We focus on practical education, simple tools, and real experiences that help people move from learning to actually using Bitcoin in their daily lives.
members.how_started:
Bitfiasi was founded by Gideon Kombian and Lydia Sena Ami Kudowor as a Bitcoin education initiative with a simple goal: help people in Ghana understand Bitcoin beyond hype and speculation. Through programs like Bitcoin for Beginners, we taught small groups and built a growing community.

But we quickly saw a gap. People could understand Bitcoin, yet had no way to use it in daily life. Education alone was not enough.

To close this gap, Bitfiasi evolved. In early 2025, we launched “Dodowa Goes Bitcoin,” shifting from just teaching to real-world use. Today, we are building a circular economy in Dodowa where Bitcoin is being introduced into everyday transactions.

What started as education is now becoming a practical, community-driven model for Bitcoin adoption, with the goal of expanding across Ghana and beyond.
members.vision: A future where Bitcoin works as real money for everyone, everywhere.
members.mission: To build a thriving Bitcoin Circular Economy in Ghana by helping communities earn, save, and use Bitcoin through practical education, local trade, and grassroots innovation.

members.name: El Zera School
members.tagline: School-based Bitcoin education initiative in South Africa building financial literacy and adoption
members.description: El Zera School is a private school in Fontainebleau, Randburg integrating Bitcoin education into the curriculum for students. We teach children about the history of money, characteristics of Bitcoin, how to acquire and safely store it, including hands-on learning with hardware wallets and seedsigner technology. By rewarding students with Bitcoin for achievements through our star chart system, we build financial literacy from an early age using positive reinforcement and practical Bitcoin experience.
members.how_started: El Zera School, led by Catherine Grobbelaar, began integrating Bitcoin education into the curriculum to provide students with early financial literacy and practical knowledge about sound money. Recognizing the importance of teaching children about Bitcoin from a young age, the school developed programs covering the history of money, Bitcoin characteristics, and self-custody practices. Students learn to create hardware wallets, work with seedsigner technology, and earn Bitcoin rewards for achievements, providing hands-on experience that transforms abstract concepts into practical understanding for the next generation.
members.vision: To raise a generation of financially literate young people who understand Bitcoin and sound money principles from an early age, equipped with practical knowledge and experience to thrive in a Bitcoin future.
members.mission: To integrate Bitcoin education into our curriculum, teaching students about money, Bitcoin fundamentals, and self-custody while providing hands-on experience through Bitcoin rewards and practical learning – building financial literacy that lasts a lifetime.

members.name: Orphans of Uganda
members.tagline: Children's home caring for orphans in Bugiri, building a new home through Bitcoin crowdfunding and Fruits-for-Sats
members.description: Orphans of Uganda Children Center (OUCC) in Bugiri District is more than an orphanage – it is a family. Founded in 2009 and now guided by former resident Isma Byarugaba, the home nourishes, educates, and protects 77 children who lack support from biological families or government sources. Through Bitcoin adoption facilitated by Bitcoin Kampala's Fruits-for-Sats initiative in 2023 and a transformative 1 BTC donation from Ray Youssef in 2025, we're building a new home for these children through crowdfunding campaigns, proving that your sats are becoming bricks.
members.how_started: Orphans of Uganda Children Center was founded in 2009 as a beacon of hope for orphaned children in Bugiri District who lack support from biological families or government sources. Now guided by Isma Byarugaba, himself a former resident, the home has cared for 77 children with unwavering commitment. In 2023, the Bitcoin Kampala community welcomed OUCC into the Bitcoin economy through the Fruits-for-Sats initiative, transforming how the organization receives and allocates donations. A year later, the community rallied to save Starlight School. In early 2025, Ray Youssef, CEO of NoOnes, donated 1 BTC, laying the foundation for a new permanent home. With support from Bitcoin Kampala and Praia Bitcoin Brazil, OUCC now runs crowdfunding campaigns demonstrating Bitcoin's power to transform charitable giving through transparency and global reach.
members.vision: To provide a loving family environment where orphaned children in Bugiri can grow, learn, and thrive with dignity – building a sustainable future through Bitcoin adoption and community support.
members.mission: To nourish, educate, and protect orphaned children who lack family or government support, providing them with food, education, and a loving home while embracing Bitcoin's transparency and global reach to ensure their needs are met with accountability and hope.

members.name: Simply Sow
members.tagline: Raising Bitcoin awareness and promoting adoption in Mossel Bay, South Africa through circular economy building
members.description: Simply Sow is a nonprofit organization in Mossel Bay, South Africa providing safety, care, and hope to vulnerable babies and children in crisis. Founded in 2022 by Thinus and Rene Mouton after fostering their first abandoned baby, we now operate a Baby Sanctuary safehouse and provide immediate care packages to children entering foster care. By accepting Bitcoin donations, we've joined a global community of supporters who believe every child deserves love, dignity, and a gentle start.
members.how_started: Simply Sow began in 2022 when founders Thinus and Rene Mouton welcomed their first abandoned baby into their home for fostering. This life-changing moment opened their eyes to the hidden reality beneath the beauty of Mossel Bay – countless vulnerable children in urgent need of safety, care, and love. What started as an effort to raise awareness and collect donations for abandoned babies quickly grew into something far greater. Today, Simply Sow operates a Baby Sanctuary safehouse dedicated to caring for infants in crisis, while also running volunteer programs and initiatives to support children, foster families, and the broader community. Entirely community-funded and relying on the generosity of donors worldwide, Simply Sow embraced Bitcoin as a way to receive support from a global network of compassionate individuals who share their vision of hope and restoration – sowing seeds of love, safety, and opportunity, one child at a time.
members.vision: Building strong foundations that inspire safe, healthy, and hopeful futures for children in need.
members.mission: Simply Sow exists to bring love, security, and hope to vulnerable babies. Through compassionate care, community support, and sustainable solutions, we ensure that every child – especially those abandoned, in foster care, or at risk – receives the love and protection they deserve.

members.name: Tri-Bitcoin
members.tagline: Leveraging triathlon's visibility to promote Bitcoin adoption, fostering community through fitness and sovereignty
members.description: Tri-Bitcoin is South Africa's first Bitcoin-powered triathlon team, established on Block height #829364. Led by Max Ng, we leverage the visibility of multisport and triathlon events to promote Bitcoin adoption, creating a community that values both physical fitness and financial sovereignty. Through racing, sponsorships, and athlete partnerships, we demonstrate that the discipline required for endurance sports aligns perfectly with Bitcoin's long-term thinking.
members.how_started: Tri-Bitcoin was established on Block height #829364 as South Africa's first Bitcoin-powered triathlon team. Founded by Max Ng, the initiative recognized a natural alignment between endurance sports and Bitcoin principles. Triathletes train for months or years, delaying gratification and maintaining discipline for a future goal – the same low time preference mindset required to understand Bitcoin. By bringing Bitcoin education and adoption opportunities to multisport events, Tri-Bitcoin introduces a community of dedicated, goal-oriented athletes to financial sovereignty. Through racing, event activations, and athlete partnerships across South Africa, the team proves that the values that make great endurance athletes also make strong Bitcoin advocates.
members.vision: To build a bridge between the triathlon community and Bitcoin, showing endurance athletes worldwide that financial sovereignty requires the same discipline, preparation, and long-term focus as their sport.
members.mission: To leverage multisport and triathlon event visibility to promote Bitcoin adoption – creating a community of athletes, organizers, and supporters who value both physical fitness and financial sovereignty.

members.name: Women of Satoshi Cooperative
members.tagline: Empowering Malawi's women with Bitcoin literacy, fostering financial sovereignty and sustainable circular economies
members.description: Women of Satoshi Cooperative empowers teenage mothers and marginalized women in Malawi through Bitcoin literacy and cooperative businesses. Led by Yankho Ngolleka, we educate 500+ women, operate ventures in maize farming, solar charging, poultry, and sunflower oil production, and run Sats Nkhonde – a Bitcoin savings cooperative. We're creating financial sovereignty pathways for women excluded from traditional banking.
members.how_started: Women of Satoshi Cooperative was founded by Yankho Ngolleka, a Certified Junior Bitcoin Developer, to address a harsh reality in Malawi: 29% of girls become pregnant before age 19, facing stigma, financial exclusion, and limited opportunities. Traditional banks demand collateral and credit histories that lock out those without capital, often driving women to predatory lenders. Ngolleka recognized that Bitcoin, as a decentralized and inflation-resistant currency, offers an alternative path to financial sovereignty – but its benefits were eluding marginalized women due to education and adoption gaps. WSC bridges this divide through a multi-faceted program: Bitcoin literacy education based on Mi Primer Bitcoin standards, four high-ROI cooperative businesses generating sustainable income, Sats Nkhonde (a Bitcoin SACCO for savings and credit), educational scholarships for teenage mothers, and vendor training to create a Bitcoin circular economy. By combining grassroots empowerment with Bitcoin innovation, WSC is proving that financial inclusion can transform lives.
members.vision: To empower teenage mothers and marginalized women in Malawi through Bitcoin education, financial inclusion, and cooperative businesses – creating sustainable self-reliance and a thriving Bitcoin circular economy.
members.mission: To lift 500+ women from poverty by providing Bitcoin literacy training, launching income-generating cooperative ventures, establishing Sats Nkhonde (a Bitcoin SACCO for savings and credit), supporting educational reintegration, and onboarding 200+ vendors to build a Bitcoin circular economy in Malawi.

members.name: Bitsavers Eduhub
members.tagline: Empowering financial freedom through Bitcoin education for young professionals and communities
members.description: Bitsavers Eduhub empowers young professionals and individuals in informal settlements with Bitcoin financial literacy, promoting financial inclusion and economic independence. Through workshops, seminars, and community outreach, we provide accessible, practical education on Bitcoin fundamentals, self-custody, security, and wealth preservation strategies. We collaborate with communities like Afribit Kibera to bridge the financial education gap and help individuals take control of their financial future using Bitcoin as a tool for economic empowerment.
members.how_started: Bitsavers Eduhub emerged from recognizing that financial exclusion remains a significant challenge in underbanked communities, particularly in informal settlements like Afribit Kibera where founder Linda Kariuki identified the urgent need for practical Bitcoin education. While Bitcoin offers a decentralized solution to traditional banking barriers, the gap between its potential and actual adoption lies in education and accessibility. Bitsavers Eduhub was created to bridge this divide by providing community-driven learning programs that make Bitcoin education accessible to those who need it most – young professionals and marginalized communities locked out of the traditional financial system.
members.vision: To empower young professionals and underbanked communities with Bitcoin financial literacy, enabling them to achieve financial independence and economic empowerment through decentralized finance education.
members.mission: To bridge the financial education gap by providing accessible, practical, and community-driven Bitcoin learning through workshops, seminars, and mentorship programs. We equip individuals with knowledge of Bitcoin fundamentals, self-custody, security best practices, and wealth preservation strategies – giving them the tools to take control of their financial future.

members.name: Lwandi Bitcoin
members.tagline: Ponta do Ouro’s Bitcoin Circular Economy: empowering our community to earn, save, and SURF toward financial sovereignty 🌊⚡
members.description:
Lwandi Bitcoin is a grassroots initiative based in the community of Ponta do Ouro Beach, Mozambique. Our mission is to build a self-sustaining, local economy powered by Bitcoin.
By leveraging the community roots of Lwandi Surf social project, the educational support of Bitcoin Famba, and the global standards of the Federation of Bitcoin Circular Economies, we empower local residents and merchants to earn, save, and spend in a decentralized currency. We are closing the loop of economic dependence, ensuring that the value generated in our coastal home stays within our community.
members.how_started:
Lwandi Bitcoin started with Lwandi Surf, a social project in Ponta do Ouro built on the belief that surfing can transform lives. While teaching the next generation to navigate the waves, it became clear they faced a different kind of "break": an economic one. High inflation and banking friction were eroding the community’s hard-earned value.

The spark ignited when the physical community of Lwandi Surf met the digital sovereignty of Bitcoin Famba. We realized that to truly protect our heritage, we didn't just need a better sport; we needed better money.

By joining the Federation of Bitcoin Circular Economies, we turned this vision into a blueprint. We are closing the loop: ensuring value generated in Ponta stays in Ponta. Today, we don't just teach kids to catch waves, we teach a community to catch the wave of financial sovereignty.
members.vision: To become a global model for how a thriving coastal community can transition from fiat-dependence to a transparent, inclusive, and resilient Bitcoin circular economy that benefits every generation.
members.mission: To empower the Ponta do Ouro community through Bitcoin education and circular trade, providing the tools for financial sovereignty, local prosperity, and the protection of our unique coastal heritage.
